description_long: This object is a vintage television with a boxy, rectangular form and a dark woodgrain exterior finish. The front features a large, slightly curved black screen set within a thick, matte black bezel, accompanied by a vertical panel of physical control buttons and a speaker grille on the right side. The control panel includes several rectangular buttons and a small red indicator light, typical of older analog televisions. The sides and back are plain, with a consistent wood-textured surface and no visible vents or ports. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a sturdy, solid build. This style of television was commonly found in homes and public spaces during the late 20th century.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, large black screen, and a side panel of physical buttons and speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large black screen, thick bezel, vertical control button panel, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, flat woodgrain surface with no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a broad, featureless woodgrain back panel.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain, flat woodgrain surface, mirroring the left side.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, matte black plastic bezel with a utilitarian, rectangular shape
attribute_control_button_layout: vertical row of rectangular buttons on the front right panel
